title: ASRA-LoRA: Adaptive Scientific Reasoning through LoRA Fine-Tuning
abstract: Most fine-tuning projects teach language models to produce better answers. ASRA-LoRA instead aims to teach models how to reason, explore, fail, revise hypotheses, and learn from experience—combining ASRA, ARC-AGI tasks, LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning, synthetic trace generation, failure-driven learning, and transition logs. The core hypothesis is that adaptive reasoning can be learned from reasoning trajectories rather than solution supervision alone. This concept paper describes modular adapters (HypothesisLoRA, ExplorationLoRA, FailureLoRA, TraceLoRA), a two-phase training roadmap on Qwen2.5 models, and benchmarking dimensions beyond task accuracy.

versions.summary: Initial SciLayer concept paper: ASRA-LoRA modular reasoning adapters, synthetic trace generation, and LoRA/QLoRA training roadmap.
